qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZIP plugin extraction allows sibling-prefix path traversal outside the intended plugin directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same vulnerable code and mechanism as the advisory: zip entry names reach `org.pf4j.util.Unzip.extract()`, which used a canonical-path `String.startsWith` containment check that can be bypassed via sibling-prefix paths during ZIP extraction.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: deletePlugin deletes plugin directories outside configured plugin roots
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is a different root cause and code path. It depends on `loadPlugin(Path)` storing an out-of-root path and `deletePlugin(String)` later deleting it, while the advisory is specifically about zip-entry traversal in `org.pf4j.util.Unzip.extract()`.

selected_finding.title: ZIP plugin extraction allows sibling-prefix path traversal outside the intended plugin directory

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory because it reaches the same vulnerable `Unzip.extract()` code and the same sibling-prefix `startsWith` bypass fixed by advisory commit `20c2f80089d1ea779e22c2de5f109a0bce4e1b14`. A fresh copied-workspace PoC run then demonstrated the advisory impact by writing a ZIP entry outside the intended extraction directory.
evidence: The advisory JSON states that PF4J before `20c2f80` has a path traversal vulnerability in the `extract()` function of `Unzip.java` due to improper handling of zip entry names.

The fix patch for `20c2f80089d1ea779e22c2de5f109a0bce4e1b14` replaces `String.startsWith()` canonical-path validation in `pf4j/src/main/java/org/pf4j/util/Unzip.java` with normalized `Path` containment checks and explicitly mentions sibling-prefix bypasses in issues `#618` and `#623`.

`archive_extraction_prefix_path_traversal/vulnerability_report.json` identifies `repo/pf4j/src/main/java/org/pf4j/util/Unzip.java:78` and describes the same source-to-sink path `DefaultPluginRepository.getPluginPaths() -> FileUtils.expandIfZip() -> Unzip.extract()`.

`.judge/poc_runs/archive_extraction_prefix_path_traversal/run.log` shows `escaped file exists: true`, `escaped content: archive-prefix-bypass`, and `SUCCESS: archive extraction wrote outside the intended plugin directory` after building the pinned vulnerable commit.

poc_executions.command: git clone https://github.com/pf4j/pf4j.git workspace/target-repo && git checkout 2ed63d16c4610779eb36f54c2e2df572a94ea2b7 && mvn -q -f workspace/target-repo/pom.xml -pl pf4j -am -DskipTests test-compile dependency:build-classpath && javac reproduction/Pf4jArchiveExtractionHarness.java && java Pf4jArchiveExtractionHarness
poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC built PF4J at commit `2ed63d16c4610779eb36f54c2e2df572a94ea2b7`, expanded `plugin-poc.zip`, reported `escaped file exists: true`, and printed `SUCCESS: archive extraction wrote outside the intended plugin directory`, confirming an out-of-root sibling-prefix write through the vulnerable ZIP extraction path.
